draft.introduction: The Mul Mantar is the opening declaration of the Guru Granth Sahib and the condensed creed of Sikhism. Every attribute of God is named in a single sentence that Sikhs consider the most concentrated expression of divine truth.

translation.text: One Universal Creator God. Truth is His Name. Creative Being Personified. Without Fear. Without Hatred. Timeless Image. Beyond Birth. Self-Existent. By the Guru's Grace.
translation.original_text: ਇਕ ਓਅੰਕਾਰ ਸਤਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਕਰਤਾ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨਿਰਭਉ ਨਿਰਵੈਰੁ ਅਕਾਲ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਅਜੂਨੀ ਸੈਭੰ ਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ

translator_notes.note: The Mul Mantar defines God through both positive attributes (truth, creativity, timelessness) and negative ones (without fear, without hatred, beyond birth). This combination of cataphatic and apophatic theology in a single sentence is distinctive. The final phrase, "by the Guru's Grace," establishes that knowledge of these attributes comes not through human reason alone but through divine revelation.
